ops.insert: GuardLink QR System — Competitive Advantage Analysis
1. The Core Thesis — The Access Barrier Is Everything
Every competitor — Everbridge, Citizen, Bond — requires an app download, account creation, or both. This single friction point eliminates 70-90% of potential reporters.
GuardLink QR system: Scan → Report → Done. 30 seconds. No app. No login. No account. No personal data collected from the reporter. Works on any smartphone camera.

Why this matters: A visitor who witnesses aggression in A&E will never download Everbridge. A retail customer who sees shoplifting will never create a Citizen account. A parent at a school event will never install Bond. But they WILL scan a QR code on the wall.

The intelligence gap: Competitors only hear from pre-registered users (staff with the app installed). We hear from EVERYONE in the building — staff, visitors, patients, parents, contractors, delivery drivers, members of the public. That's 5-10x more intelligence per site.

Anonymous by design: Reporters don't need to identify themselves. This removes fear of retaliation — Culture Shift 2026 research shows UK employees are scared to speak up. QR anonymity solves this at the architecture level, not the policy level.

Zone-aware: Each QR code is linked to a specific zone/location. When someone scans it, the system automatically knows WHERE the report came from — no GPS tracking, no location permissions needed. The physical placement of the QR IS the geo-intelligence.

Deployment speed: Print QR posters. Stick them on walls. System is live. No IT integration. No MDM rollout. No staff onboarding sessions. No app store approvals. 48 hours from contract to operational.
2. Everbridge — Mass Notification for the Boardroom
Everbridge 360 is a Critical Event Management (CEM) platform. Three tiers: Core, Professional, Enterprise. Taken private by Thoma Bravo in 2023 for $1.5B.

Core function: Mass notification — sends alerts to thousands of employees via SMS, email, voice call, push notification, and desktop pop-up. Used by Fortune 500, government agencies, and public safety (IPAWS integration for national alerts in US).

Visual Command Center aggregates threat intelligence feeds (weather, civil unrest, terror alerts) onto a global map. Security operations teams monitor the dashboard and decide when to broadcast alerts.
Customers include Sanofi, Goldman Sachs, HSBC, Siemens. Claims 358% ROI. Recognised as Forrester Wave Leader in CEM.

REQUIRES: Everbridge mobile app download (iOS/Android) + account creation + organisation registration code. Employees must download the app, create an account, and enter their organisation's unique code.

Employees must provide personal mobile numbers to receive alerts. This creates a GDPR friction point — staff may refuse to share personal numbers. Organisations then have to issue company phones or accept coverage gaps.
Visitors, contractors, patients, and members of the public have ZERO access. They cannot report. They cannot receive alerts. They are invisible to the system.
Implementation: 6-12 months for enterprise deployment. Requires dedicated admin team. Per-user licensing means every new employee needs provisioning.
UX described as 'workflows buried under layers of clicks' — designed for security operations professionals, not frontline shop workers or nurses.
Pricing — Why They're Not Our Market
Enterprise deployments (10,000+ contacts with multiple modules): $100,000-$500,000+/year. Setup fees, customisation, training all extra. GSA pricing shows 6.85% setup fee on annual contract value.
Our Starter tier at £199/mo (£2,388/yr) is 6-25x cheaper than Everbridge's smallest deployment. Our Professional at £499/mo is still 1/10th the cost. And we deploy in 48 hours, not 6-12 months.
What Everbridge Cannot Do (That We Can)
No in-the-moment response guidance — broadcasts alerts but never tells the person what to DO
No QR-triggered reporting — every report requires a logged-in user with the app
No anonymous reporting channel — reporter identity is always known to the system
No de-escalation training or situational scripts
No building-level zone heatmap — their Visual Command Center shows macro threats (earthquakes, weather, unrest), not which corridor in your hospital has the most aggression incidents
No AI triage of individual incidents — no ARIA equivalent
No voice journal / anonymous wellbeing channel
No UK safeguarding compliance engine (Martyn's Law, KCSIE, CQC, HSE)

"Everbridge is excellent at what it does — telling your security team about macro events. But can I ask: when a member of the public threatens your receptionist at 6pm on a Tuesday, what does Everbridge tell HER to do? ... Nothing. Because it's not designed for that. We are."

"We don't compete with Everbridge. We complete what Everbridge can't do. They handle the broadcast. We handle the person. Many of our clients run both — Everbridge for mass alerts, Absolute Defence for frontline protection."

"And respectfully — you're paying six figures a year for a system your frontline staff probably don't even have installed on their phones. Our QR code works for everyone in the building. Including the visitor who just witnessed something your staff missed."
3. Citizen — The Street Scanner That Stops at the Door

Citizen is a consumer safety app with 10M+ users. It monitors police/fire radio dispatch and CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) feeds in US cities and pushes real-time location-based alerts to nearby users.
Claims to deliver alerts up to 45 minutes before traditional news or emergency systems. Covers 20+ incident categories (shooting, fire, robbery, assault, missing person, etc).
Users can livestream incidents — creating a crowdsourced video surveillance network. Citizen now pays users to go live on the platform. NYPD can access user-generated videos.
Recently expanded to 15 new US cities (March 2026). Launched Citizen Enterprise — feeds alerts into business tools like Slack/Teams for corporate security teams to monitor asset zones.
Targeting $42M in latest fundraising round (PitchBook). Enterprise pricing undisclosed — demo/quote required.
REQUIRES: App download (iOS/Android) + account creation + location permissions enabled. Must allow continuous background location access for alerts to work.
US-only. No UK police/fire radio feeds. No UK emergency service integration. No UK regulatory compliance. Useless in every sector we target.
Monitors PUBLIC incidents (street-level crimes, fires, accidents) from emergency dispatch. Has zero visibility into what happens INSIDE buildings, offices, hospitals, schools, or retail stores.
Crowdsourced content risk — user-generated reports and livestreams can spread misinformation. Has faced criticism for inciting vigilante behaviour and causing panic from unverified alerts.
What Citizen Cannot Do (That We Can)
No response guidance — tells you WHAT is happening nearby but never tells you what to DO about it
No internal incident reporting — designed for public/street-level events only
No anonymous reporting channel — all reports tied to user accounts
No compliance engine — no audit trail, no HSE/Ofsted/CQC reporting, no Martyn's Law alignment
No building/zone-level intelligence — cannot tell you which corridor, ward, or floor has problems
No training, no SOS protocol, no wellbeing channel, no AI triage, no acknowledgement chain, no UK presence
"Citizen is a street scanner. It tells you there's a stabbing three blocks away. Useful — but it can't tell your nurse what to do when an aggressive patient is IN her ward right now. We can."

"Citizen doesn't operate in the UK. It has no integration with UK emergency services. It produces no compliance data your regulator would accept. And it requires every user to download an app and share their location permanently."

"The fundamental difference: Citizen watches the street outside your building. We protect the people inside it. They aggregate public incidents. We manage YOUR incidents — with guidance, accountability, and a compliance trail."
Bond is an AI-powered 'Preventative Personal Security' platform. NASDAQ-listed as OBAI (Feb 2026). $100M+ invested. 28 countries. 1.4M+ security service requests handled. App Store 4.8/5 rating.

Combines AI with 24/7 human Personal Security Agents in command centres. Core features: Ready An Agent (panic hold — press and hold screen to put a security agent on standby), Video Monitor Me (live video watched by a human agent), Track Me On The Go (journey monitoring to destination), Send Me A Car (dispatches rideshare), Siren (phone alarm with lights/sound), Location Services (family real-time tracking).

Heavyweight advisory board: former US Secret Service director, former NYPD commissioner, former FBI deputy director, former Israeli VIP protection head. Uses this for credibility with enterprise buyers.
Enterprise wins: Top-3 US telecom, $300B+ pharma leader, trillion-dollar multinational, European fintech firms. $250K ARR from single pharma deal. Potential $10M+ ARR from largest pilot.
REQUIRES: App download (iOS/Android) + account creation + subscription payment. Every feature requires the user to have the app open and active on their phone.
Consumer pricing: Basic (free — siren + location only). Premium $50/mo ($450/yr). Premium Plus $100/mo ($900/yr). Enterprise pricing is per-employee, custom quote.
At $50/mo per user, protecting 200 retail staff costs $120,000/year. For the same estate, Absolute Defence costs sub-£5,000/year. That is a 24x cost difference.

Designed for INDIVIDUALS outside buildings — walking home, commuting, lone travel. Not designed for organisational premises protection. Has no concept of building zones, site maps, or organisational risk patterns.

Human-agent model doesn't scale cost-effectively. Every 'Ready An Agent' or 'Video Monitor Me' session requires a paid human security agent in the command centre watching a live feed. This model works for executives — it does NOT work for 500 retail workers or 10,000 NHS staff.
Market cap ~$27M despite $100M+ invested. Stock has fallen 94.91% from 52-week high. The market is questioning whether the personal bodyguard-on-call model is economically viable.
This validates our approach: AI-first, QR-triggered, no human agent bottleneck. Our unit economics scale. Theirs don't.
What Bond Cannot Do (That We Can)
No QR-triggered reporting — every interaction requires app + active subscription
No organisational intelligence — protects individuals, not buildings or zones
No de-escalation guidance or situational response scripts
No training component — zero behavioural change capability
No anonymous internal reporting — no Voice Journal equivalent
No compliance engine — no audit trail, no UK regulatory alignment (Martyn's Law, HSE, KCSIE, CQC)
No building-level heatmap or zone risk scoring
No AI triage (ARIA equivalent) — relies on expensive human agents for every escalation

"Bond is a personal bodyguard app for individuals. They protect one executive walking home. We protect EVERY person in your building — staff, visitors, patients, contractors — with a QR code on the wall. No app download. No subscription per person."

"At Bond's pricing, protecting your 200 staff costs £120,000 a year — and that's just the app subscription, before anyone calls a human agent. Our entire platform for the same estate costs under £5,000. And ours includes training, AI triage, heatmaps, and compliance data. Theirs doesn't."

"Bond has ex-Secret Service advisors. We have operators who've actually been in hostile environments. They raised $100M building a personal bodyguard app and the stock is down 95%. We built a complete organisational threat intelligence system for a fraction — because we know what actually works in the field."
5. Real-World Scenarios — Who Protects the Person in the Moment?
Scenario A: Aggressive Customer in a Retail Store
Everbridge: Does nothing. This is a micro-incident, not a mass event. The shop worker has no guidance. No report is filed unless she logs into a separate HR system later.
Citizen: Does nothing. This is inside a building. No police dispatch was triggered. Citizen has no visibility.
Bond: Only helps if the worker has the app installed AND a paid subscription AND opens it mid-confrontation. Then a human agent watches via video. No de-escalation script. No organisational record.

Absolute Defence: Worker scans QR in back office → gets de-escalation guidance for aggressive customer scenario (legal position, verbal techniques, when to disengage). If it escalates, SOS button dials 999 and creates silent timestamped log. Another customer who witnessed it scans the public QR poster and files an anonymous report. ARIA triages both submissions. Heatmap updates. Area manager is notified. Acknowledgement chain starts counting.
Scenario B: Violent Patient in NHS A&E
Everbridge: Could broadcast a mass alert to hospital staff, but ONLY if a security team operator sees it first and decides to trigger one. The nurse being threatened gets nothing.
Citizen: Does nothing. No police radio dispatch triggered yet. Even if it was, Citizen doesn't operate in the UK.
Bond: Nurse would need to pull out her personal phone, open the Bond app, and initiate a video call with a security agent mid-assault. Completely impractical. No organisational incident log created.

Absolute Defence: QR on the ward wall gives instant guidance for violent patient scenario. SOS triggers silent alert to trust security team with GPS-stamped ward location. Waiting room visitor scans public QR to report what they witnessed. ARIA cross-references with previous incidents on same ward. Heatmap shows A&E night shift as highest-risk zone. Trust has full audit trail for CQC inspection.
Scenario C: Suspicious Person at a School Event
Everbridge: School doesn't have Everbridge — £100k+ annual contract is beyond any school budget. Even if it did, parents and visitors have no access.
Citizen: Does nothing. Not available in UK. No police dispatch triggered. A suspicious person isn't a 911 call.
Bond: Parent would need Bond installed + paid subscription. Even then, Bond connects them to a remote human agent who has no context about the school's safeguarding procedures.

Absolute Defence: Parent scans QR poster at school entrance. Reports suspicious person in 30 seconds — anonymous, no app, no account. Designated Safeguarding Lead receives immediate notification. ARIA flags the report and cross-references with any previous concerns about the school perimeter. If critical, SOS escalation path activates. Full KCSIE-compliant chronological log created automatically. Ofsted can see exactly how the school responded and when.
Scenario D: Lone Estate Agent — Property Viewing Turns Hostile
Everbridge: Does nothing. This is a lone worker scenario in a private property — completely outside Everbridge's mass notification model.
Citizen: Does nothing. Inside a property. No public dispatch.

Bond: This is Bond's strongest scenario. If the agent has the app, they can press Ready An Agent or initiate Video Monitor Me. A human security agent watches via live video. Bond can dispatch a car or coordinate with police. However — this costs the estate agency $50-100/mo per agent, there's no organisational intelligence, and no de-escalation guidance.

Absolute Defence: Agent was trained in hostile encounter de-escalation (Layer 1). Has SOS one-tap panic on phone (no app — bookmarked QR link). Situational Guide gives step-by-step response for 'hostile person in confined space' scenario. SOS creates silent GPS-stamped log with auto-999. Office manager receives instant notification. Pattern data shows this is the third incident at evening viewings — training and policy adjusted. Duty of care documented for SRA/FCA compliance.
App download required? — Everbridge: YES | Citizen: YES | Bond: YES | Absolute Defence: NO
Account creation required? — Everbridge: YES | Citizen: YES | Bond: YES | Absolute Defence: NO
Personal data collected from reporter? — Everbridge: YES (mobile number) | Citizen: YES (account + GPS) | Bond: YES (account + payment) | Absolute Defence: NO (anonymous by default)
Visitors/public can report? — Everbridge: NO | Citizen: Only public street incidents | Bond: NO | Absolute Defence: YES
Time to report? — Everbridge: 5-10 min (login + navigate) | Citizen: N/A (reporting not core function) | Bond: 2-3 min (open app, connect agent) | Absolute Defence: 30 seconds (scan QR, type, submit)
Deployment time? — Everbridge: 6-12 months | Citizen: Days (alerts only) | Bond: Days (app rollout) | Absolute Defence: 48 hours (full system)
Annual cost for 200-person site? — Everbridge: £15k-60k+ | Citizen Enterprise: Undisclosed (demo required) | Bond: ~£120k ($50/user/mo) | Absolute Defence: sub-£5k
In-the-moment response guidance? — Everbridge: NO | Citizen: NO | Bond: NO (calls human agent) | Absolute Defence: YES (Situational Guide + ARIA)
UK regulatory compliance? — Everbridge: Partial (ISO only) | Citizen: NO (US only) | Bond: NO | Absolute Defence: YES (Martyn's Law, KCSIE, CQC, HSE, ISO 45001)
Behavioural training component? — Everbridge: NO | Citizen: NO | Bond: NO | Absolute Defence: YES (military-grade progressive training)
"Everbridge tells your security team what happened. We tell the PERSON IN THE MOMENT what to do. And we cost 1/50th."
"Citizen watches the street outside. We protect the people inside. They don't even operate in the UK."
"Bond protects one person with a $50/month app. We protect every person in the building with a QR code on the wall."
"They all require an app download. We require a QR code on the wall. That's why we hear from everyone — and they only hear from people who bothered to install their software."
"Everbridge broadcasts. Citizen scans. Bond bodyguards. We DEFEND. Training + Platform + Intelligence. No one else does all three. That's Threat Intelligence for Ordinary People."
